title: The Executor Worker
concept: Running a focused loop to complete one sub-task at a time, feeding results back to the planner.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: what does a Task Planner do with a big user request?

options.text: It breaks the request into a list of smaller sub-tasks
It deletes the request and starts over
It answers instantly without any steps

exercises.explain: The Task Planner takes one big goal and decomposes it into a clear, ordered list of smaller sub-tasks so they can be tackled one at a time.

cards.title: The plan is ready... now what?
cards.body: A planner made a to-do list of sub-tasks. But a list doesn't do anything by itself. Something has to actually roll up its sleeves and DO each task.

cards.title: The Executor Worker
cards.body: The Executor is the part of an agent that takes ONE sub-task at a time and completes it. When it finishes, it reports the result back to the planner before moving on.

cards.title: Like a construction crew
cards.body: The planner is the site manager with a blueprint. The Executor is the worker who builds one wall, checks it off, then asks 'what's next?'. One focused job at a time.

cards.title: Planning a trip
cards.body: Plan: 1) find flights, 2) book hotel, 3) make itinerary. The Executor first ONLY searches flights, hands back the results, then moves to the hotel. It never juggles all three at once.

cards.title: Myth: it does everything at once
cards.body: Nope! The Executor stays focused on a single sub-task. Doing one thing well and reporting back keeps mistakes small and easy to catch.

cards.title: One loop at a time
cards.body: The Executor runs a tight loop: take a task, do it, report the result, repeat. The results flow back so the planner always knows where things stand.

left.text: Holds the full plan and decides the order of sub-tasks.

right.text: Completes one sub-task, then feeds the result back.

exercises.prompt: What does the Executor Worker do after finishing a sub-task?

options.text: Feeds the result back to the planner and moves to the next task
Throws away the result and stops
Starts all remaining tasks at the same time
Rewrites the entire original plan by itself

exercises.explain: The Executor completes one task, reports the result back so the planner stays informed, and then takes the next sub-task in the list.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The Executor works on many sub-tasks at the same time to save effort.
exercises.explain: False. The Executor stays focused on ONE sub-task at a time. That focus keeps its work accurate and easy to check.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about the Executor's job.
exercises.sentence: After completing a sub-task, the Executor feeds the result back to the planner.
exercises.explain: The Executor sends the result of each finished sub-task back to the planner, so the planner always knows what's been done.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence sounds like it was written by an AI describing an Executor Worker?

options.text: The Executor Worker systematically processes each sub-task in sequence, returning outputs to the orchestrating planner for continuous progress tracking.
It just does one job then tells the boss it's done.
My robot did the flight thing first, then hotel.

exercises.explain: The first option is polished, formal, and full of tidy jargon like 'systematically' and 'orchestrating planner' — a classic AI-generated style.

exercises.prompt: A planner lists: 1) get weather, 2) pick outfit, 3) pack bag. How should a proper Executor handle this?

options.text: Do task 1, report the weather result, then do task 2, and so on — one focused loop at a time
Guess all three answers instantly without checking anything
Skip reporting results so the planner can't interfere
Combine all three tasks into one giant messy step

exercises.explain: A good Executor runs a focused loop: complete one sub-task, feed its result back to the planner, then move to the next. This step-by-step teamwork keeps the whole agent accurate and on track.
